get.description:
Dynamically discovers and lists available API endpoints.

Introspects the URL configuration to find all registered endpoints.
Shows only direct children under the current path (not all descendants).
Uses the same traversal order as Django's URL resolver, so the first
matching pattern wins (like `show_urls --unsorted`).

Usage:
    path('v1/', APIRootView.as_view(view_name='v1'), name='v1-root'),
    path('', APIRootView.as_view(view_name='my_project'), name='index'),

    path('', APIRootView.as_view(view_name='my_project'), name='index'),

Usage:
    path('v1/', APIRootView.as_view(view_name='v1'), name='v1-root'),

get.description:
Role Definitions (roles) contain a list of permissions and can be used to
assign those permissions to a user or team through the respective
assignment endpoints.

Custom roles can be created, modified, and deleted through this endpoint.
System-managed roles are shown here, which cannot be edited or deleted,
but can be assigned to users.

get.description:
General data about models and permissions tracked by django-ansible-base RBAC

Information from this endpoint should be static given a server version.
This reflects model definitions, registrations with the permission registry,
and enablement of RBAC features in settings.

allowed_permissions: Valid permissions for a role of a given content_type

Use this endpoint to get a list of users who have access to a resource.
This is a list-only view that provides a list of users, plus extra data.

get.description:
This gives drill-down information about the means of inheritance
for all the permissions show in the higher-level view of the access list

Use this endpoint to give a team permission to a resource or an organization.
The needed data is the team, the role definition, and the object id.
The object must be of the type specified in the role definition.
The type given in the role definition and the provided object_id are used
to look up the resource.

After creation, the assignment cannot be edited, but can be deleted to
remove those permissions.
get.x-ai-description: Retrieve a RBAC role grants assigning permissions to team for specific resources

parameters.name: order

get.description:
This gives drill-down information about the means of inheritance
for all the permissions show in the higher-level view of the access list

get.x-ai-description: Retrieve a RBAC role grants assigning permissions to user for specific resources

post.description: Give a user permission to a resource, an organization, or globally (when allowed). Must specify 'role_definition' and exactly one of 'user' or 'user_ansible_id'. Can specify at most one of 'object_id' or 'object_ansible_id' (omit both for global roles). The content_type of the role definition and the provided object_id are used to look up the resource. After creation, the assignment cannot be edited, but can be deleted to remove those permissions.

allOf.description: Must provide exactly one of: user (integer ID) or user_ansible_id (UUID). Mutual exclusivity enforced by server validation.

user.description: ID of the user to assign the role to. Mutually exclusive with user_ansible_id.

user_ansible_id.description: Ansible ID of the user to assign the role to. Mutually exclusive with user.

allOf.description: Can provide at most one of: object_id or object_ansible_id. Omit both for global role assignments. Mutual exclusivity enforced by server validation.

object_id.description: ID of the resource object. Mutually exclusive with object_ansible_id.

object_ansible_id.description: Ansible ID of the resource object. Mutually exclusive with object_id.

post.x-ai-description: Create a RBAC role grants assigning permissions to user for specific resources

parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this role user assignment.

post.description:
Manage Organization objects in the 'organization' relationship of this particular Team.

Starting from the detail URL:

GET /:id/organization/ to show organizations currently in the relationship

POST a list of instances to /:id/organization/associate/ to add those organizations to the relationship

POST a list of instances to /:id/organization/disassociate/ to remove those organizations from the relationshp
post.x-ai-description: Associate organization with a team

GET /:id/organization/ to show organizations currently in the relationship

POST a list of instances to /:id/organization/disassociate/ to remove those organizations from the relationshp
post.x-ai-description: Disassociate organization from a team

ChartDataItem.description: Serializer for a single time-series data point (timestamp label and integer value).

label.description: Label for the data point (e.g. timestamp)

value.description: Value for the data point (e.g. number of job runs)

api_slug.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

service.description: service namespace to track what service this type is for. Can have a value of shared, which indicates it is synchronized.

app_label.description: Django app that the model is in. This is an internal technical detail that does not affect API use.

model.description: Name of the type according to the Django ORM Meta model_name convention. Comes from the python class, but lowercase with no spaces.

parent_content_type.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

pk_field_type.description: Database field type of the primary key field of the model, relevant for interal logic tracking permissions.

api_slug.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

codename.description: A codename for the permission, in the format {action}_{model_name}. Where action is typically the view set action (view/list/etc) from Django rest framework.

content_type.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

created.description: The date/time this resource was created.

created_by.description: The user who created this resource.

operation.description:
The type of change recorded by the entry (i.e. create/update/delete/etc).

* `create` - Entity created
* `update` - Entity updated
* `delete` - Entity deleted
* `associate` - Entity was associated with another entity
* `disassociate` - Entity was disassociated with another entity

changes.description:
We store strings, we have to convert them back to the correct type.
Related associations and disassociations will show a null for changes.

content_type.description: The content type which was changed in this entry.

object_id.description: The object id which was modified in this entry.

related_content_type.description: The content type if this entry is recording an many to many (M2M) change.

related_object_id.description: The object id of the related model if this entry is for an many to many (M2M) change.

name.description: The name of the feature flag. Must follow the format of FEATURE_<flag-name>_ENABLED.

FilterOptionWithId.description: Serializer for a single filter dropdown option with an integer ID and display name.

FilterSet.description: Serializer for FilterSet, exposing name, filter criteria, and default flag.

id.description: ID of the filter set entry

filters.description: JSON object containing the filter criteria

is_default.description: Indicates whether this filter set is the default for the user (only one default per user allowed)

FilterSetRequest.description: Serializer for FilterSet, exposing name, filter criteria, and default flag.

filters.description: JSON object containing the filter criteria

is_default.description: Indicates whether this filter set is the default for the user (only one default per user allowed)

Report.description:
Serializer for per-template aggregated report rows.

Each row represents a single job template with aggregated run counts, elapsed
time, cost estimates, and savings calculations across the selected date range.

time_taken_manually_execute_minutes.description: Estimated time to perform this task manually (minutes)

time_taken_create_automation_minutes.description: Estimated time spent creating this automation (minutes)

runs.description: Total number of times this job template was executed

elapsed.description: Total elapsed time for all runs (seconds)

elapsed_str.description: Total elapsed time for all runs (human-readable string)

automated_costs.description: Estimated costs of running this job template on AAP

manual_costs.description: Estimated costs if all jobs were run manually

time_savings.description: Estimated time savings in seconds by automating this job template

time_savings_str.description: Estimated time savings by automating this job template (human-readable string)

savings.description: Estimated cost savings by automating this job template

ReportChart.description: Serializer for a chart series including the time granularity kind and data items.

kind.description: Type of date range for the series (e.g. hour, day, month, year)

items.description: Data points for the chart series

ReportDetail.description:
Serializer for the dashboard detail endpoint response.

Aggregates total run counts, cost figures, time savings, unique host count,
top users, top projects, and chart series data for the selected date range.

total_number_of_successful_jobs.description: Total number of successful job runs

total_number_of_failed_jobs.description: Total number of failed job runs

total_number_of_host_job_runs.description: Total number of host job runs (sum of all hosts across all jobs)

total_saving.description: Total savings from automation (manual costs - automated costs)

total_time_saving.description: Total time savings from automation (in hours)

total_number_of_unique_hosts.description: Total number of unique hosts across all job runs

top_users.description: List of top users who executed the most jobs

top_projects.description: List of top projects associated with the most job executions

job_chart.description: Chart data showing job executions over time

host_chart.description: Chart data showing host job runs over time

Task.description:
Main serializer for Task model with comprehensive functionality.

This serializer provides task creation, scheduling, and monitoring
capabilities for the dashboard interface and includes all fields
needed for task management.

function_name.description: Name of the function to execute (must match TASK_FUNCTIONS)

task_data.description: JSON data to pass to the task function

scheduled_time.description: ISO 8601 format datetime when task should run

is_system_task.description: Whether this is a system-defined task that cannot be easily deleted

result_data.description: JSON result data from task execution

can_retry.description: Check if task can be retried.

can_delete.description: Check if task can be deleted.

can_modify.description: Check if task can be modified.

is_ready_to_run.description: Check if task is ready to run.

next_run_time.description: Get next run time for recurring tasks.

created.description: The date/time this resource was created.

modified.description: The date/time this resource was created.

Serializer for task cleanup operations.

This serializer handles the cleanup functionality from manage_tasks.py.

days.description: Number of days to keep completed tasks

dry_run.description: Show what would be deleted without actually deleting

command with validation and proper data handling.
